summary: High‑energy UMass show with soaring Jack Straw and stellar Terrapin Station
blurb: The set opens with a bright Jack Straw and rolls through tight takes of Tennessee Jed, Deal and Good Lovin'. The second set shines with an on‑fire Terrapin Station → Playing In The Band segue, crisp Drums → Space, and emotive Stella Blue. Jerry’s guitar work is “toppin’” while Brent’s fresh keyboards add extra sparkle.
review: On May 12 1979 the Grateful Dead turned Alumni Stadium into a rain‑soaked playground for an electric spring concert. From the opening “Jack Straw,” where Garcia’s merry lines already hinted at the night’s high voltage, through a punchy first set that kept the tempo driving—thanks in large part to Bill and Mickey’s relentless double‑drum push—the band built momentum that exploded in the second set.  Highlights include a soaring “Terrapin Station” that segues into an expansive “Playing In The Band,” a tight Drums → Space jam, and a soulful “Stella Blue” that feels almost reverent amid the stormy crowd energy.  Jerry was described as “on another level” and “toppin’,” with Phil’s bass equally locked in, while Brent’s richer keyboard textures earned praise for hopping and adding depth to the mix.  The audience’s chaotic enthusiasm, amplified by the wet‑field atmosphere, gives this show a raw but joyous feel that makes it a must‑listen for any Deadhead looking for a quintessential late‑’70s performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All five recordings are flagged positive; reviewers repeatedly use phrases like “on another level,” “stellar,” “toppin’,” and praise the entire second‑set flow, indicating strong consensus of exceptional playing across the show.

best_recording.reason: Clear, well‑balanced soundboard capture with minimal bleed; preserves the rain‑soaked energy and delivers the most reliable listening experience.

key_highlights: Soaring “Jack Straw” that sets a high‑energy tone despite the rain.
Second‑set firework: Terrapin Station → Playing In The Band segue, crisp Drums → Space jam, and heartfelt Stella Blue.
Brent’s richer keyboard textures add new sparkle to classic material.
Rain‑soaked crowd chaos adds an authentic live atmosphere.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers merry, on‑another‑level solos throughout; his lead work on the second set is described as “toppin’” and a highlight of the night.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass provides a solid, humpin' foundation that matches Jerry’s intensity, earning praise for being ‘on another level.’
band_performance.Bob: Weir contributes abundant rhythm‑guitar energy; while one reviewer notes only a disapproving look at a biker, others see him as part of the group that prevailed.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s new keyboard textures are richer and more lively, with reviewers calling his playing “hopping” and adding depth to the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart drives the early pace and blends tightly with Bill during the Drums → Space segment, delivering a spirited double‑drum performance.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann pushes the tempo from the first verse, provides a tight ride, and flows seamlessly into Space, contributing to the show’s high energy.
